arts.ttl: Exclusive: Midfield 'magician' wanted by Tottenham and Man United tells his club he's definitely leaving
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur have had an up-and-down season so far and, while they look promising, there’s still work to be done. Meanwhile, Manchester United are at...

arts.ttl: Rodri proud to be first City player to win Ballon d’Or
arts.sum: “It’s more than a dream come true to win the Ballon d’Or,” Rodri said in his City Studios interview. “At City, of course you have team-mates, you have the coach, also the people who work in the club that don’t appear in the lights but they are there and they help you every day,” he added.

arts.ttl: Rodri: Coping with injury has been difficult
arts.sum: “But I was keeping in touch with the team-mates and watching all the games - suffering sometimes. “But always I have a positive mentality to go again and recover well.

arts.ttl: Rodri: I can’t wait to share Ballon d’Or success with the fans
arts.sum: The first City player to win it and on Saturday I will be with them and I will enjoy this with them,” he declared. And Rodri says he’s relishing the chance to show gratitude to the supporters who he says have shown him love from his first day at the Club.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola is "probably the best manager to have ever existed on Earth", says Erling Haaland
arts.sum: Manchester City striker Erling Haaland explains what makes Pep Guardiola the best manager in the world and "probably the best to have ever existed on Earth".

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola makes Man City relegation promise amid 115 Premier League charges
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola insists he will remain Manchester City manager even if the club are relegated over alleged breaches of financial rules.

arts.ttl: Simon Jordan makes Man City APT point as Premier League vote outcome revealed
arts.sum: The latest Manchester City news as the pundit gave his thoughts of the situation ahead of Friday's vote over the rules.

arts.ttl: Football Daily | Pep Guardiola, APT and a mixed day for Manchester City
arts.sum: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! “I’m not moving, I can assure you,” husked Pep Guardiola in the social media drop announcing his contract extension until 2027. The clip focuses on Pep’s crow’s feet and coal-black eyes, each line possibly marking the efforts required to win the multiple trophies he has brought Manchester City. Still, in bringing the news City fans so craved, it was understated, low-budget even, in these times of people living their best lives on Insta-disgraces with the production values of Kevin Costner’s vehicle Waterworld. The English National Opera, which is decamping from London to Manchester, is promising projects bringing together opera and football. I see opportunities here for an entirely new, star-studded international operatic repertoire, featuring ‘Don Carlos Tevez’, ‘The Marriage of Figo’, ‘Thuramdot’ and, for local City fans, ‘Tosca Bobb’. And presumably there’ll be a penalty aria – Adrian Irving. Re. this on the LDV Vans Auto Windscreen trophy (yesterday’s News, bits and bobs): ‘And the Bristol Street Motors Trophy is being rebranded – mid-competition, no less – to the Vertu Trophy. You’ll doubtless be reassured the EFL has provided a pronunciation guide: VUR-CHOO.’ Seems like typical Vurchoo-signalling to me – Mark Read. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Neymar aims thinly-veiled dig at Rodri after the Man City star urged Vinicius Jnr to 'pay attention on the field' after beating him to Ballon d'Or
arts.sum: There was controversy on the night as Real Madrid and their star Vinicius opted to boycott the ceremony after the Brazilian was snubbed, with Rodri commenting on the situation this week.
